title: 3D interpolation of irregular data with a POCS algorithm
abstract: We present a Projection Onto Convex Sets algorithm for 3D interpolation of irregularly sampled seismic data. The method alternates between thresholding the F-K spectrum and re-imposing known traces, converging to a band-limited reconstruction without requiring user-tuned regularization weights.

title: Simultaneous seismic data denoising and reconstruction via multichannel singular spectrum analysis
abstract: Multichannel singular spectrum analysis (MSSA) reorganizes f-x slices into block Hankel matrices whose low-rank structure encodes coherent events. Truncated SVD then both denoises and reconstructs missing traces in one pass.

title: Simply denoise: wavefield reconstruction via jittered undersampling
abstract: We exploit curvelet sparsity for both denoising and reconstruction of seismic wavefields. Combined with jittered undersampling, l1-promoted curvelet recovery outperforms regular subsampling and band-pass denoising.

title: Seismic data interpolation through convolutional U-Net
authors: Wang, B., Liu, N., Chen, Y.
abstract: We adapt the U-Net encoder-decoder for seismic 2D interpolation, training on synthetic gathers with random trace masks. Skip connections preserve high-frequency content, achieving SNR gains of 5–8 dB over POCS on the Mobil AVO benchmark.

title: Seismic data interpolation using generative adversarial networks
authors: Li, J., Chen, X., Wang, Y.
abstract: We propose a conditional GAN for seismic trace interpolation. The discriminator constrains the generator toward phase-consistent reconstructions, addressing the over-smoothing common in MSE-trained networks.

title: Random noise attenuation by a deep residual learning network
abstract: We retrain a deep residual CNN (DnCNN) on synthetic Marmousi gathers contaminated with additive Gaussian noise. The network predicts the noise component, with batch normalization stabilizing training under varying input SNRs.

title: SeisFormer: a window-attention transformer for seismic processing
authors: Chen, K., Xu, P., Zhao, L.
abstract: SeisFormer adapts the Swin transformer with shifted window attention along the trace axis. By capturing long-range trace coherence missed by purely convolutional models, it sets new SOTA on the Mobil AVO Viking Graben benchmark.

title: Seismic denoising as a diffusion-based inverse problem
authors: Park, S., Kim, J., Lee, H.
abstract: We frame seismic denoising as posterior sampling under a score-based diffusion prior. A measurement-consistency term steers the reverse SDE, recovering low-amplitude reflections that discriminative networks miss at -5 dB input SNR.

title: First-arrival picking with a U-shaped convolutional network
authors: Hu, L., Zheng, X., Liu, Y.
abstract: PickNet is a 1D U-shaped CNN that predicts a Gaussian-shaped likelihood of first-break arrival per sample. It improves over STA/LTA by ~20 percentage points in accuracy at low SNR.

title: PhaseNet: a deep-neural-network-based seismic arrival-time picking method
abstract: PhaseNet is a U-Net trained on three-component waveforms to output per-sample P/S phase probabilities. While developed for earthquake catalogs, it transfers cleanly to active-source first-break picking.

title: Unrolled POCS networks for seismic interpolation
authors: Müller, A., Schmidt, R., Becker, T.
abstract: We unroll N iterations of POCS into a trainable network where each thresholding step is replaced with a learned proximal operator. The hybrid retains data-consistency guarantees while matching CNN reconstruction quality.

title: SeisBERT: a pre-trained foundation model for seismic data processing
authors: Xu, Z., Wang, J., Zhou, R.
abstract: SeisBERT pre-trains a masked-trace transformer on 50 TB of unlabeled marine and land surveys, then fine-tunes per task. A single backbone reaches near-SOTA on interpolation, denoising, and first-arrival picking with as few as 100 labeled examples.
